commit.message:
Fix game hang by correcting CPU branch timing

Branch taken (+1) and page-crossing (+2) cycle penalties were lost because
branch() incremented this.cycles but execute() overwrote it with the base
cost. Use a branchPenalty field that gets added after execute() returns.
Also remove temporary debug instrumentation from PPU and NesEmulator.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add component diagram in draw.io format

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix game hang after screensaver by delivering APU frame counter IRQs to CPU

The APU's isIrqPending() was never checked in the main loop, so games
relying on APU IRQs for timing (e.g. Double Dragon & Battletoads) would
spin indefinitely waiting for an interrupt that never fired.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add APU implementation for sound support

Implement NES Audio Processing Unit with pulse, triangle, and noise
channels, wired into the CPU bus and main emulation loop. Audio output
uses javax.sound.sampled at 44100 Hz.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix game freeze after title screen by adding NMI edge detection, PRG RAM, and DMA NMI handling

- Trigger NMI immediately when game enables NMI via PPUCTRL while VBlank is active
- Add 8KB PRG RAM at $6000-$7FFF so game state reads/writes work correctly
- Check for NMI during DMA transfer to avoid missing VBlank interrupts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add README with architecture overview, setup, and API docs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement notification system with server cluster, workers, emulators, and load testing

- Notification server: REST API, input validation, template rendering, Redis caching, RabbitMQ routing
- Notification worker: 4 dedicated queue consumers (APNS/FCM/SMS/EMAIL) with retry logic
- Third-party emulator: simulates delivery services with 95% success rate
- Device emulator: receives notifications, WebSocket broadcast, REST stats
- Load client: seeds test data, multi-threaded load test with p95/p99 reporting
- Infrastructure: PostgreSQL, Redis, RabbitMQ, Prometheus, Grafana with pre-provisioned dashboard
- Docker Compose with service health checks and replicas
- 27 unit tests covering validation, services, controllers, consumers
- Component diagram in drawio format

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add game research, plan generation, and scaffolding tools

Game names are never stored in this repo — they are read at runtime from
each project's local resources/name.txt (gitignored in each game repo).

Tools added:
- game_research.py: Opus + web search → rules/experience/strategy markdown
- generate_game_plan.py: PDF + generic template → game-specific plan
- scaffold_game.py: create React project from standard template
- research_all_games.py: discover and research all games dynamically

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(docker): remove working_dir override and fix UID mismatch (#5)

Bug 1: docker-compose.yml set working_dir: /workspace which overrode
the Dockerfile WORKDIR /app, causing "Cannot find module
/workspace/dist/server.js" on startup.

Bug 2: Dockerfile created a custom agent user (UID 999) but
docker-compose defaulted to UID 1000 (node user), causing silent CLI
failures due to permission errors on /home/agent/.claude/.

Fix: Remove working_dir, switch Stage 3 to use the existing node user
(UID 1000) matching the dev stage, and update all /home/agent
references across docs and code.

Also removes misleading "KNOWN ISSUE" comment about CLI streaming —
the actual cause was the UID mismatch, not a CLI bug.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add venv/ to .gitignore so UPDATE_APP.bat doesn't delete it

git clean -fd was wiping the venv directory on lite installs
because it wasn't gitignored. Portable installs were unaffected
since python_embeded/ was already ignored.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Feature/t355 permanent keyframe selection (#198)

* T355: Fix framerate mismatch breaking keyframe selection

Replace hardcoded `useState(30)` framerate in useCrop and useHighlight
with derived value from `videoMetadata.framerate`. On non-30fps videos,
all frame calculations were wrong (e.g., 60fps endFrame at 600 was
searched at 300), making keyframes unselectable.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* T355: Fix permanent keyframe selection when seek is clamped by trim

When permanent keyframes exist outside the trim range (frame 0 before
trim start, endFrame after trim end), clicking their diamonds seeks to
the trim boundary instead. The time-based selection then fails because
currentFrame doesn't match the keyframe's frame.

Fix: track clicked keyframe index via ref, use as fallback when
findKeyframeIndexNearFrame returns no match after a clamped seek.
Cleared on playback start and when a time-based match succeeds.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* T355: Update task doc with actual root cause findings

The primary issue was trim clamping (not framerate mismatch). Permanent
keyframes outside the trim range couldn't be selected because seek was
clamped to the visible range, making time-based selection fail.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* T355: Improve deselection - track settled time after clamped seek

The previous fix kept the clicked keyframe selected even when the user
seeked elsewhere. Now tracks the clamped currentTime at the moment of
fallback use, and clears the selection once currentTime moves away by
more than 1 frame. This ensures permanent keyframes deselect correctly
when the user clicks elsewhere on the timeline.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* T355: Fix keyframe diamond hit area - add transparent bg so clicks register

The invisible hit area div had no background, causing click events to pass
through it to elements behind. Added bg-transparent and expanded from
-inset-2 to -inset-3 for a larger click target (~36x36px vs ~17x17px).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* T355: Select boundary keyframes when clicking beyond edge zones

Clicking past the last keyframe on the crop track now selects the end
keyframe; clicking before the first selects the start keyframe. This
makes permanent keyframes easier to select since their diamonds sit
at the timeline edges.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* T355: Select boundary keyframes when playhead is at trim boundaries

Permanent keyframes sit at full video boundaries (frame 0 / endFrame)
but the playhead clamps to trim boundaries. Added a fallback in
selectedCropKeyframeIndex: when the playhead is at a trim boundary,
select the corresponding permanent boundary keyframe.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

ॐ CRITICAL: fix world-wipe race condition (Alexandria bug)

The nuclear bug: on server restart / HMR / page reload, the Zustand store
initializes with empty arrays (no objects, no transforms, no lights).
The active world ID comes from localStorage IMMEDIATELY. If ANY store action
triggers saveWorldState() before loadWorldState() completes its async fetch
from Supabase, the empty default state overwrites the real world data.

Fix: _worldReady guard flag. saveWorldState() refuses to write until
loadWorldState() has completed at least once. Also:
- loadWorldState() now calls cancelPendingSave() before starting
- switchWorld() blocks saves during transition
- createNewWorld() respects the guard
- enterViewMode() respects the guard

This was a silent data-destruction bug — no error, no warning, just gone.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Update PROGRESS.md for session 2 completion

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix three bugs causing 0% occupancy and raw IDs everywhere

Bug 1 — /api/pricelabs/stats wrong field names:
  Stored keys are occ_next_30/recommended_base but endpoint was reading
  occupancy_next_30/recommended_base_price → all values null → 0% everywhere.

Bug 2 — calcFeedOcc no fallback for legacy events:
  Events synced before feed_key was added have no feed_key field.
  calcFeedOcc's strict === match found nothing → 0/30d for all bars.
  Fix: detect if any events in dataset have feed_key; if not, fall back to
  propId-based count distributed evenly across feeds for that property.
  Collapsed the old f.feedKey ternary into a single calcFeedOcc(feedKey, propId, ...) call.

Bug 3 — stale store data (no canonical_name, no occ fields):
  Store was populated with old-format data before canonical_name field existed.
  Fix: startup_pl_ical() runs 8s after boot — refreshes PriceLabs listings
  (stores canonical_name + occupancy fields) then immediately re-syncs all
  iCal feeds (tags every event with feed_key). Runs on every deploy.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: resolve 422 CSRF error on first login after account setup

HTTP Origin header (http://) didn't match request.base_url (https://)
because assume_ssl was enabled without force_ssl. Enable force_ssl to
redirect HTTP→HTTPS before form submissions, and auto-login users
after password setup to skip the manual login step entirely.

Look up LeaseBuilder data to re-render signed PDFs with inline signatures

Instead of checking lease.notes (which is empty for builder leases),

form data JSON. This allows builder-generated leases to be re-rendered
with signature images placed directly on the signature lines.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Program.Run to be idempotent when process is already running (#24)

* Fix Program.Run to be idempotent when process is already running

ExecuteProject starts the program before iterating evaluators, but each
evaluator also calls program.Run at the start. Without an idempotency
guard, each call launched a new OS process sharing the same stdin pipe,
causing commands to be consumed by the wrong instance.

Add a `running` bool to Program that is set true on successful Start and
cleared on Kill. Run returns immediately (no-op) if already running.

Also add tests covering the idempotent Run and the Kill+Run restart cycle.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Preallocate required slice in openai.go (prealloc)

* Fix pre-existing lint issues exposed by golangci-lint v2.11.2 upgrade

- Replace httptest.NewRequest with httptest.NewRequestWithContext throughout
  test files (noctx: authn, ipextract, logging, requestid, version, server)
- Remove now-unused nolint:gosec directive in rubric_server.go (nolintlint)
- Preallocate required slice in openai.go (prealloc)

* Address PR review comments

- Kill: only clear running flag when cleanup succeeds or process already exited,
  preventing a failed Kill from allowing a second process to spawn via Run
- Tests: mark IdempotentRun and RunAfterKill as noParallel to avoid os.Chdir
  races in setup
